name: Baked Tomatoes Stuffed with Vegetables
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Using tomatoes held at room temperature until fully ripe, cut a 1/4 inch thick slice from the stem end of each.
3. With a spoon, carefully scoop out pulp from each tomato; reserve the pulp.
4. Arrange tomatoes in an 8-inch square pan and set aside.
5. In a medium skillet, melt 1 tablespoon of margarine.
6. Add eggplant, carrot, green pepper, mushrooms, onion, garlic, and reserved tomato pulp to the skillet. Cook until vegetables are tender and most of liquid has evaporated, about 5 minutes.
7. Stir in dill, oregano, salt, and black pepper.
8. Fill tomatoes with vegetable mixture, mounding slightly in the center.
9. Dot remaining margarine over the tomatoes.
10. Pour hot water into the baking dish to a depth of 1/2 inch.
11. Cover and bake until tomatoes are tender, about 30 minutes.
12. Remove tomatoes carefully with a slotted spoon.
13. Serve.
